Does Money actually buy happiness? A statistical analysis
between GDP and happiness level.

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Recognisable investigative structure (introduction â data â methods â results â conclusion).
Clear headings, contents table and bibliography give a sensible framework.
weaknesses: Sections are fragmented with repeated phrases and mis-labelled or incomplete subsections.
Noticeable repetition and digressions reduce relevance and disrupt coherence; proofreading errors persist.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report shows an overall logical path and adequate organization (Band 3 for structure) but coherence is only partial and transitions are abrupt (Band 2 for logical development), with unnecessary repetition (Band 2 for relevance). On balance the work best fits a Band 2 profile.

strengths: Correct use of standard statistical notation (r, ÏÂ², df, Hâ/Hâ, regression formula) in most places.
Integration of tables, contingency table and scatterplot supports the discussion effectively.
weaknesses: Inconsistencies in rounding, equation labeling and formatting reduce clarity.
Occasional vague statements (e.g. ellipses instead of detailed steps) and category definitions mismatch earlier text.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly appropriate (Band 3) and representations are used effectively (Band 3), but communication suffers from inconsistencies and abrupt narrative shifts (Band 2). Overall this aligns with a Band 2 performance.

strengths: Initial reference to the studentâs Egyptian context provides a slight personal connection.
weaknesses: Approach follows a generic sequence of standard tests with limited personal insight.
No novel data handling or creative methodology; independent thinking and initiative are minimal.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands of personal engagement reflect only limited evidence of independent thinking, personal approach and creativity, placing the work firmly in Band 1.

strengths: Acknowledges that factors beyond maths may influence happiness.
weaknesses: Reflection is superficial, lacking methodological critique or depth.
No detailed analysis of test assumptions or connection to enhanced mathematical understanding.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection remains at a basic level across depth, critical analysis and connection to understanding, matching Band 1 descriptors.

strengths: Purposeful selection of multiple statistical tools (correlation, ÏÂ², regression) within the SL syllabus.
Good conceptual explanations of hypotheses, degrees of freedom and interpretation of r and ÏÂ² results.
weaknesses: Computational and transcription mistakes in formulas and arithmetic (e.g. denominator miscalculation in r, mis-copied GDP values).
Inconsistent arithmetic precision (e.g. b-values) and formula presentation errors affect accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs course-level mathematics effectively (Band 4 for relevance) and shows good understanding throughout (Band 4), but errors in computation and accuracy reduce correctness to mostly correct (Band 3). A Band 3 award reflects this balance.

strandSelections.reasoning: The choice to compare GDP and happiness is common and the statistical procedures are standard textbook methods.  Limited personal insight is visible beyond initially citing the Egyptian context, so evidence of independent thinking is only limited (Band 1).

Explanations of statistical concepts (hypotheses, degrees of freedom, meaning of r) indicate good conceptual grasp across the exploration, even if not rigorously deep.  This demonstrates good understanding throughout, meriting Band 4.

Most numerical results are plausible, but there are computational or transcription mistakes (e.g. mis-copied GDP values, inconsistent denominator in r-formula).  Therefore mathematics is mostly correct rather than fully correct, Band 3.

Most paragraphs relate directly to the research question, but there is noticeable repetition of statements (e.g. duplicated explanation of the râvalue) and some digressions about national morale that are not taken up later in the analysis.  Hence the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ, corresponding to Band 2.

Standard statistical notation ( r, ÏÂ², df, Hâ, Hâ, y = mx + b ) is used correctly in most places, variables are defined and formulae are typeset.  A few slips appear (e.g. Î£(xiâx)Â² +(yiây)Â² under a single root), yet overall notation is mostly appropriate throughout, meriting Band 3.

Explanations are generally understandable but inconsistencies in rounding, mismatched equation labels, and abrupt shifts between narrative and calculation reduce clarity.  Communication is therefore âmostly clear with some consistencyâ, Band 2.

Correlation, chi-square and linear regression are all within the AI SL syllabus and used appropriately to address the question.  The selection is purposeful and covers more than one statistical technique, indicating effective use, thus Band 4.

The student provides tables, a contingency table, and a scatterplot with regression line; each is integrated in the text and supports the discussion.  While not sophisticated, these multiple representations are used effectively, aligning with Band 3.

Reflection is confined to brief comments that other factors must influence happiness.  There is no deeper consideration of methodological limitations or data reliability, so reflection is limited (Band 1).
No novel data handling or creative modelling techniques are attempted; the work repeats standard procedures.  Consequently, only some minor initiative is present, placing it in Band 1.

The investigation follows a recognisable path (introduction â data â methods â results â conclusion).  Nevertheless, several sections are fragmented (e.g. repeated phrases, incomplete sentences, mis-labelled sub-sections) and the logical bridge between the three statistical tools is not always explicit.  Overall coherence is present but transitions are sometimes abrupt, so the work best matches the descriptor for âsome coherence and logical developmentâ (Band 2).
The analysis merely states whether tests are significant; it does not interrogate assumptions (e.g. linearity, interval data requirements).  Thus critical analysis remains basic, warranting Band 1.
The student links results to the research question, but the connection to enhanced mathematical understanding is superficial.  Hence Band 1 is appropriate.

Clear headings, a contents table and a final bibliography give the report a sensible structure.  Introduction, body and conclusion are identifiable and placed in a logical order.  Minor issues such as page references that do not match, spelling errors in headings and a missing separate appendix prevent it from being âexceptionallyâ organised, therefore Band 3 is appropriate.

Although the topic is introduced through the studentâs personal background, the subsequent approach follows a generic sequence of statistical tests without a distinctive personal drive.  This fits Band 1 for personal approach.

annotations.comment: The fragment âbetween GDP and happiness level.â lacks context; integrate it into the main title or introduction to improve coherence.
The table of contents contains a spelling error (âconcluciosnâ) and mismatched page references; proofread headings and verify page numbering for consistency.
The introduction is highly descriptive but lacks a precise research question; explicitly state your hypothesis or question to guide the investigation.
This paragraph ends abruptly, suggesting a missing continuation; ensure each section is complete and logically structured.
The description of data collection lacks specific source citations inline; include precise references (e.g., URLs with access dates) for transparency.
The title and subtitle appear disjointed; consider merging into a coherent, single phrase that clearly states the research question.
The criterion âEconomic diversificationâ is sound, but the grouping method is vague; illustrate how you categorized countries quantitatively.
The data table repeats header rows and is difficult to navigate; consolidate headers and consider splitting into smaller tables or using filters for clarity.
You list variables clearly, but could include definitions of symbols (e.g., Î£) for readers unfamiliar with notation; this enhances mathematical communication.

The formula is correctly stated, but in later steps the denominator is miscalculated as a sum rather than a product under the root; ensure you follow $$\sqrt{\sum(x_i - \bar x)^2 \sum(y_i - \bar y)^2}$$.
â*similar steps are done for the rest of the calculationsâ is vague; summarise key values rather than deferring to ellipses to maintain clarity and reproducibility.
The summation values are given without units or context; specify what each sum represents (e.g., Î£(x_i - x)(y_i - y) for 50 data points).

The square-root of Î£(yi - È³)^2 is computed correctly, but the combined denominator later is shown as $$\sqrt{\Sigma(x_i - x)^2 + (y_i - y)^2}$$ which conflicts with the original formula; this major error affects r-value accuracy.
The scatterplot lacks axis labels, units, and a descriptive caption; add these to improve interpretation.
This narrative repeats the r-value interpretation twice in one sentence; remove redundancy and link to significance testing to deepen analysis.
Hypotheses are stated, but consider clarifying the null and alternative in words (e.g., âno associationâ) and aligning with research context.
âHighâ category is defined inconsistently with earlier text; ensure category boundaries match exactly.
Expected frequency table shows inconsistent decimal formatting (e.g., â10.0â vs â6.80â); standardise to two decimal places.
Calculation of E_{(1,1)} is clear, but demonstrate at least one more cell to show generality of the method.
The computation uses (10 - 6.8)^2 = (10.24)^2 which is incorrect; it should be (3.2)^2 = 10.24; re-evaluate these terms for accuracy.
Application of Yates' correction is appropriate, but you should state why correction is necessary (e.g., expected frequency <5) to reflect critical understanding.
You report p = 0.035 without stating Î±-level; explicitly mention Î± = 0.05 and interpret p-value relative to it.
The conclusion restates results but offers no critical assessment of test limitations (e.g., sample size); include these reflections to deepen analysis.
There is a small inconsistency: b calculated as 5.74 but reported as 5.73; ensure precise arithmetic and matching values.
The final conclusion summarises methods but lacks a clear statement on the reliability and predictive power of the regression model; reflect on RÂ² and residuals.
This summary repeats earlier findings without new insight; consider integrating personal interpretation or real-world implications to demonstrate engagement.
While you acknowledge other factors, reflection on how these could be incorporated or tested would strengthen critical analysis and future direction.
References lack consistent formatting (missing authors, publication details, access dates); follow a standard citation style (e.g., APA) for presentation.
